description_long: This is a large, multi-story research institute building with a rectangular footprint and a flat, slightly textured roof. The facade features rows of evenly spaced, rectangular windows with blue-tinted glass, framed by white structural elements. The building is elevated on a platform with a prominent staircase leading to the main entrance, which is set back under a wide overhang. The side elevations are mostly solid, with minimal fenestration and a series of circular protrusions adding visual interest. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from concrete, with glass used for the windows and metal likely present in window frames and structural reinforcements. The overall design is functional and modern, with clean lines and minimal ornamentation.
description: A modern, multi-story research institute with blue-tinted windows, a flat roof, and a prominent entry staircase, constructed mainly from white concrete and glass.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with four rows of blue-tinted windows and the central entry platform with stairs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the solid wall with three circular protrusions and minimal windows.
description_view_2: Rear or alternate facade with similar window arrangement as the front, but with a longer platform and a staircase to the right.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the solid wall with circular features and the entry platform with stairs.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway is centrally located on the front facade, accessed by a wide staircase leading up to a recessed platform beneath a large overhang. The entrance appears to be double-doored, likely made of glass or metal, and is accessible via the stairs, but no ramps or additional accessibility features are visible. The entry is unadorned, with no visible decorative elements.
